title: Tool Specification Abstraction
summary: Eliminate ~80% of code duplication across 42 tool implementations by introducing a declarative ToolSpec pattern

problem.description: Every tool implements nearly identical boilerplate for ensure(), add_handler(), and platform-specific install functions
evidence: Each new tool requires 50-80 lines of copy-paste
Bug fixes require updates across all tool files
problem.impact: ~4,200 lines that could be reduced to ~500 lines

functional.description: Declarative tool definition via data structures, not code
Central package name registry mapping tool names to PM-specific package names
Platform support flags to declare which platforms a tool supports
Custom install hooks for complex tools (nvm, rustup) that need override logic
Auto-registration of tools without manual mod.rs edits

non_functional.description: Zero runtime overhead vs current approach
Backward compatible with existing tool implementations

technical_approach.selected: Option A - Struct-based with thin macro wrapper
technical_approach.rationale: Type-safe, IDE support, compile-time validation with ergonomic macro syntax

tasks.title: Create ToolSpec structs
tasks.description: Create src/tools/spec.rs with ToolSpec, MacOsInstall, LinuxInstall, and WindowsInstall structs
acceptance_criteria: ToolSpec struct with name, command, version_flag fields
Platform-specific install structs with package manager options
All fields are &'static str for zero-cost abstraction
Structs derive Debug, Clone where appropriate

subtasks.title: Define ToolSpec base struct
subtasks.description: Create the main ToolSpec struct with name, command, version_flag, and platform install options

subtasks.title: Define MacOsInstall struct
subtasks.description: Create struct with homebrew and cask options

subtasks.title: Define LinuxInstall struct
subtasks.description: Create struct with apt, dnf, yum, zypper, pacman, apk options

subtasks.title: Define WindowsInstall struct
subtasks.description: Create struct with winget and choco options

tasks.title: Implement ToolSpec methods
tasks.description: Add ensure(), install(), and handler() methods to ToolSpec
acceptance_criteria: ensure() checks version satisfaction and calls install if needed
install() dispatches to platform-specific package manager
handler() returns closure compatible with registry
Methods use existing common.rs utilities (cmd_satisfies, run, has)

subtasks.title: Implement is_installed() method
subtasks.description: Check if tool command exists and satisfies version requirement

subtasks.description: Install via Homebrew formula or cask

subtasks.title: Implement install_linux() method
subtasks.description: Install via detected package manager (apt, dnf, pacman, etc.)

subtasks.title: Implement install_windows() method
subtasks.description: Install via winget or chocolatey

subtasks.title: Implement ensure() method
subtasks.description: Main entry point that checks and installs if needed

subtasks.title: Implement into_handler() method
subtasks.description: Convert ToolSpec to registry-compatible handler function

tasks.title: Create define_tool! macro
tasks.description: Create ergonomic macro in src/tools/macros.rs that expands to ToolSpec
acceptance_criteria: Macro accepts concise syntax: define_tool!(name, { ... })
Expands to full ToolSpec struct with all fields
Supports optional platform blocks
Provides clear compile errors for invalid input

subtasks.description: Finalize the ergonomic syntax for define_tool! macro

tasks.title: Create package name registry
tasks.description: Create src/tools/packages.rs with central mapping of tool names to PM-specific package names
acceptance_criteria: Single source of truth for all package name mappings
Compile-time hash map using phf crate
Covers all existing tools' package name variations
Easy to add new mappings

subtasks.title: Add lookup helper function

tasks.title: Add custom install hook support
tasks.description: Allow ToolSpec to specify custom install function for complex tools
acceptance_criteria: ToolSpec has optional custom_install field
Custom function takes precedence over standard install
Type signature compatible with existing tool installers
nvm, rustup, and other complex tools can use this

Existing tests continue to pass

Code reduced by ~80% per tool

risks.description: Complex tools need escape hatch for custom logic
risks.mitigation: Allow custom_install function pointer in ToolSpec
Run full test suite after each tool migration
Keep macro thin, most logic in regular functions
Test on all platforms before committing to crate choice

internal: PRD-004 (Semantic Version Checking) - provides version_satisfies() used by ToolSpec
